opp.next_step: 6/29 - No Material Update. 6/22 - Following up with Amresh to receive updated evaluation timeline, No response yet 6/1 - No material update 5/13 - Amresh is back in office. Will follow up with some available times to meet week of 5/18 4/28 - Coordinating TIme with Amresh to schedule a demo with additional stakeholders. Amresh is out of office till 5/8
opp.ais_why:
• Champion Amresh Munshi engaged the August 19 2025 demo squarely on autonomous AI. The call confirmed strong interest in autonomous sourcing and AI-enabled negotiation, with explicit configurable thresholds for purchases below $25,000. He set savings expectations of 4 to 9 percent from autonomous negotiation, framing AI as the value driver rather than a feature on the wishlist.
• Amresh and ops lead Purav Desai treated AI as the core architecture. The integration requirement was that Zycus deliver an overlay solution on Oracle iProcurement, Oracle Fusion and ServiceNow without ripping iProcurement, with mandatory TSR review automation for sub-$25K critical software. That is buyer-led specification of where AI must plug in.
• Project sequencing reinforces the Hungry call: exploration through September, POC in December or January, go-live targeted May 2026 within roughly a year. Amresh is convening additional stakeholders including Chris and Alicia and power users. Two recorded sessions, one completed and one cancelled, plus action items committing both sides to AI-anchored next steps.

items.quote: my head of global procurement to be part of it to give his blessing and take it forward
items.source: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi

sources: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi
metrics.narrative: On the 19 August 2025 demo Amresh Munshi set a concrete savings expectation of 4 to 9 percent from autonomous negotiation ('assume that 4% is a savings target', with headroom to 6 to 9 percent) and quantified the risk driver, citing a roughly $10,000 add-on tool bought below threshold that bypassed the mandatory TSR review, which he called 'a security breach'. The pain is real and partly numbered, but no validated ROI or total-savings model was built with the buyer, and the numbers are now 10.5 months stale with no reconfirmation. Building a quantified savings/risk case with procurement ops (Purav Desai) is the reactivation move.

sources: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi
champion.narrative: Amresh Munshi (self-described project lead) was a strong emerging champion on 19 August 2025: 'this is really exciting, and we want to implement it', he praised Michael's leadership, and he committed to convene procurement ops, IT, power users and his head of global procurement. But he has been unresponsive for about 10.5 months, ignoring Clari nudges (March to June 2026) and roundtable invites, so the champion is now cooling to dark and unconfirmed as still active or still employed on the project. He also gate-kept every other thread, leaving Zycus single-threaded. What developed: genuine enthusiasm and detailed requirements; what is missing: sustained engagement, an EB introduction, and any second internal advocate.

sources: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi
competition.narrative: On 19 August 2025 Amresh said Cornerstone was 'exploring all the providers' and would 'onboard one or two of them to go for the POC', naming a parallel evaluation against GEP Worldwide, iValua, JAGGAER and SAP Ariba (Salesforce Competitors__c). Oracle iProcurement/Fusion is the entrenched incumbent that stays in place as the ERP/P2P baseline, so the contest is for the agentic-AI source-to-pay overlay, not a rip-and-replace. After 10.5 months of silence and a lapsed September 2025 down-select, the single strongest current threat is do-nothing / a stalled or deprioritized initiative rather than any named rival. Zycus's wedge, if the deal reopens, is the Merlin autonomous-sourcing and negotiation demo the buyer already praised, plus the Oracle-overlay fit and Tata Sky/TASL proof points.

sources: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well established and owned by Amresh Munshi. Cornerstone has no source-to-pay system, so tail-spend supplier outreach happens 'outside the system, but via email' with no audit trail; there is Maverick-spend and process-compliance exposure; and a real TSR/security breach already occurred when a roughly $10,000 sub-threshold tool bypassed IT security review, raising PII and data-privacy concerns. Amresh also framed Oracle as a structural pain: iProcurement is locked into the Fusion ERP package ('take it or leave it') and Oracle is 'behind in enhancing' agentic capability at 'very significantly high cost', a legacy of a 2021 decision to move off NetSuite and drop Coupa that he called a 'mistake'. This is confirmed operational and financial pain, not inferred.

sources: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are only lightly sketched. Amresh described a two-step commercial path on 19 August 2025 (partial contracting to start the POC, then final contracting about one month after POC completion) and noted that legal review, contract management, IT security and privacy reviews all run through ServiceNow internally. No MSA/SOW timeline, procurement channel, InfoSec questionnaire, or contract-expiration forcing function has been established. Visibility here will only come once the evaluation restarts and a mutual timeline is co-authored.

sources: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi
SF OpportunityContactRole (Chris Soriano, VP of Strategic Sourcing)
economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is unmapped. Amresh Munshi stated on 19 August 2025 that he is 'the project lead' but that 'my head of global procurement' must 'give his blessing and take it forward' and that he would not engage senior people 'unless I don't see a value out of it', so he is gatekeeping access to budget authority. That head of global procurement has never been named or introduced, and Chris Soriano (VP of Strategic Sourcing per Salesforce), the most senior mapped contact, has never engaged despite a 25 June 2026 outreach. Naming and reaching the budget owner is a prerequisite to any revival.

sources: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi
decision_process.narrative: Amresh laid out an explicit process on 19 August 2025: explore all providers through September 2025, then finalize identification and pricing, down-select 1 to 2 vendors for POC, run the POC to December 2025/January 2026, partial contracting during the POC with final contracting about a month after POC completion, then implementation to a May 2026 go-live (with a safer roughly one-year fallback). He named the eventual committee: procurement ops (Purav Desai), IT, power users, and his head of global procurement. The process design is confirmed, but execution has entirely stalled and none of these gates is verified as reached, so the real current process state is unknown.

sources: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi
decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer articulated clear, specific criteria on 19 August 2025: an autonomous, agentic-AI overlay that sits on top of Oracle iProcurement and Oracle Fusion ERP plus ServiceNow WITHOUT replacing the P2P layer ('we cannot replace iProcurement... what we are looking for is source to pay as well as the autonomous AI based agentic AI on top of it'); configurable value thresholds routing sub-$25,000 tail spend to autonomous sourcing; mandatory TSR-review auto-flagging for new software/AI purchases even below threshold; Microsoft Teams and Okta SSO (SAML 2.0) integration; and strategic-sourcing analytics to arm negotiators on high-value SaaS/data-center deals. These are buyer-led specifications, not inferred. They remain valid but must be re-confirmed given the elapsed time.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: A competitor is pushing back — Recency-weighted, the strongest CURRENT threat is do-nothing / a stalled initiative: the buyer's own September 2025 down-select, the December 2025/January 2026 POC and the May 2026 go-live all passed with 10.5 months of silence, so the deal is at real risk of defaulting to no decision. The named field (GEP Worldwide, iValua, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba) reflects a parallel evaluation the buyer described on 19 August 2025 but whose outcome was never shared, so those rivals sit at carried-forward medium threat pending fresh evidence. Oracle iProcurement/Fusion is the incumbent that stays as the ERP baseline (Zycus overlays it, does not replace it), a low current threat but the integration constraint. No new competitive evidence surfaced this run; threat levels are held, not re-ranked.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: Cornerstone OnDemand — Formal Evaluation · $185K · closes 27 Aug

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 22 is carried by momentum 55 vs stage-expected 52; business case strength +0.30 (weight 10). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 55 (moving forward): Next Step carries 7 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 6 is anchored on the stage win (22), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 55, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 7 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score__c 8.0, AIS_Status__c Strong. The 19 August 2025 call corroborates it strongly: the buyer specified where agentic AI must plug in (sub-$25k autonomous sourcing, TSR auto-flag, negotiation thresholds), treating AI as the core value driver rather than a wishlist feature. The field is well earned, though the appetite is now 10.5 months unconfirmed.

business_case.evidence: Buyer articulated a 4-9% autonomous-negotiation savings target and concrete risk drivers (Maverick spend, a ~$10k tool that bypassed TSR review = 'a security breach', PII exposure), but no validated ROI/savings model was co-built and it is 10.5 months stale.

deal_movement.summary: No buyer-side movement since the 19 August 2025 demo. Field history 2026 is entirely internal: CloseDate slipped three times (25 Mar to 17 Apr to 4 Jun to 27 Aug 2026), Amount trimmed 210k to 185k (18 Mar 2026), Stage moved Shortlisted to Formal Evaluation and forecast Upside Key Deal to Pipeline (14 Apr 2026, both by Michael McCarthy), Division reclassified to Mid-Enterprise (29 Jun 2026). Every recent Next_Step edit is a rep note logging non-response.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 25 July 2025, qualified same day. Last verified buyer engagement 19 August 2025 demo call (about 318 days / 10.5 months before the 3 July 2026 sweep). last_activity_date 26 June 2026 is rep-side only (roundtable invites and Clari nudges, no buyer reply). Stage Formal Evaluation, close 27 August 2026 (55 days out). Buyer's own 19 August 2025 plan (explore to September 2025, down-select 1 to 2 vendors for POC, POC December 2025/January 2026, go-live May 2026) is fully lapsed with zero confirmed progress. Dark far beyond the buyer's stated cadence is a hard-kill signal at this stage.
evidence: Last verified buyer engagement: 19 August 2025 demo call, about 10.5 months before this sweep
All rep touches since (Clari emails 9 Mar, 25 Mar, 7 Apr, 24 Apr, 22 Jun 2026; roundtable invites 18-26 Jun 2026) drew no buyer reply

Buyer's planned end-September 2025 vendor down-select outcome never shared
Planned December 2025/January 2026 POC never confirmed to have started
Planned May 2026 go-live is now past with no contract
Economic buyer (Amresh's 'head of global procurement to give his blessing', 19 Aug 2025) never named or engaged
Chris Soriano (VP Strategic Sourcing), a required stakeholder Amresh named on 19 Aug 2025, never engaged; roundtable invite 25 Jun 2026 unanswered
Close date 27 August 2026 (55 days) is indefensible for a Formal Evaluation deal dark for 10.5 months
north_star_verdict.headline: Cold roughly 10.5 months after a strong 19 August 2025 demo; every buyer-stated milestone (September 2025 down-select, December 2025/January 2026 POC, May 2026 go-live) passed with no confirmation, and multiple rep outreaches March to June 2026 went unanswered.

flags: Single-threaded on Amresh Munshi, who has been silent about 10.5 months; open parallel threads to Purav Desai (Evaluator) and Chris Soriano (VP Strategic Sourcing) now.
Economic buyer (Amresh's head of global procurement) was never named or engaged; identify and reach the budget owner to have any path to close.
Every buyer-stated milestone (Sept 2025 down-select, Dec 2025/Jan 2026 POC, May 2026 go-live) lapsed; re-qualify that the project is still funded and active before forecasting it.
best_practice_check.summary: Deal is single-threaded on a dark champion with an unmapped economic buyer and a lapsed evaluation timeline; the win-strategy priority is to re-open multiple threads and re-qualify whether the initiative is still live.

customer_preference.evidence: Strong praise for the Merlin demo on 19 August 2025 ('really exciting, we want to implement it'), but the buyer also said 'we are exploring all the providers' and has since gone dark ~10.5 months, so the preference signal has decayed.

The named field (GEP Worldwide, iValua, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba) reflects a parallel evaluation the buyer described on 19 August 2025 but whose outcome was never shared, so those rivals sit at carried-forward medium threat pending fresh evidence. Oracle iProcurement/Fusion is the incumbent that stays as the ERP baseline (Zycus overlays it, does not replace it), a low current threat but the integration constraint. No new competitive evidence surfaced this run; threat levels are held, not re-ranked.

competitors.name: Do-nothing (status quo / stalled initiative)
competitors.quote: 10.5-month silence since the 19 August 2025 demo; buyer's planned September 2025 down-select, December 2025/January 2026 POC and May 2026 go-live all lapsed with no confirmation and no reply to March-June 2026 outreach
competitors.how_we_win: Re-engage Amresh Munshi, or thread to Purav Desai and Chris Soriano, anchoring on the August 2025 pain (no source-to-pay, Maverick spend, the TSR/PII breach) and the cost of continued inaction to make status quo untenable.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.quote: we are exploring all the providers to see who can best fit into our requirement, and the player who is having more capability... will onboard one or two of them to go for the POC
competitors.source: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi; SF Competitors__c
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with Merlin autonomous sourcing and agentic negotiation maturity plus MS Teams/Okta SSO and Oracle/ServiceNow overlay fit; emphasize configurability and user experience versus GEP's heavier platform.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.quote: we are exploring all the providers to see who can best fit into our requirement
competitors.source: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi; SF Competitors__c
competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin agentic AI, autonomous negotiation, and strategic-sourcing analytics depth as the AI-first tail-spend overlay versus iValua's broader but less AI-mature suite.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.quote: we are exploring all the providers to see who can best fit into our requirement
competitors.source: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi; SF Competitors__c
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with Merlin autonomous AI and intake simplicity plus the Oracle-overlay fit tailored to Cornerstone's Oracle Fusion/ServiceNow stack.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: we are exploring all the providers to see who can best fit into our requirement
competitors.source: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi; SF Competitors__c
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast Zycus's AI-first architecture and overlay simplicity against Ariba's legacy complexity and cost; leverage Amresh's stated aversion to expensive large-suite lock-in (his 2021 Oracle Fusion 'mistake').

competitors.name: Oracle iProcurement / Fusion (incumbent)
competitors.quote: we cannot replace iProcurement... it is integrated with our Oracle ERP system, which is Oracle Fusion... Oracle has clearly said we are behind in enhancing that thing, and if we do it, it will be very significantly high cost, and we don't want to spend in Oracle right now
competitors.source: Avoma demo call 19 August 2025, Amresh Munshi
competitors.how_we_win: Overlay architecture: sit on top of Oracle iProcurement and Fusion without replacing them, delivering autonomous sourcing, Merlin intake, and TSR auto-flagging that Oracle cannot provide at acceptable cost.

Zycus is well positioned on the AI narrative and Oracle-overlay fit; the deal's failure mode is disengagement, not weak AI positioning.

• Project sequencing reinforces the Hungry call: exploration through September, POC in December or January, go-live targeted May 2026 within roughly a year.
